page: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 shared vCPUs mapped to 16 physical AMD EPYC Genoa cores running at 2.0 GHz, supported by a 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided by a 320 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this configuration.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in specific areas. The single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng, ranks in the top tier (top 10%), while multi-core performance falls into the average category. Memory performance is notable, with PassMark memory scores reaching the top 10% tier and large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths ranking in the top 25%. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks (gzip and bzip3) also show strong top-25% performance, whereas multi-threaded decompression and database operations align with average mid-tier expectations.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, representing a standard general-purpose configuration. The shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention tradeoffs compared to dedicated-core instances, though the underlying AMD EPYC Genoa architecture mitigates this with advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration. The inclusion of local NVMe storage provides a cost-efficient alternative to external block storage for workloads requiring direct disk access.

This server is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and moderate database operations. The combination of strong single-core performance, high memory bandwidth, and local NVMe storage makes it appropriate for web application hosting, caching layers, and software compilation tasks that do not require continuous, dedicated multi-core CPU utilization.
description: The Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM and 320 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server delivers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core CPU performance and database operations remain in the average tier, reflecting its shared CPU allocation. Lacking a dedicated GPU, this instance is a cost-efficient option for general-purpose applications, static web serving, caching, and moderate database workloads requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 320 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd features 16 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 320 GB NVMe SSD storage for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring shared AMD EPYC Genoa processors, high-speed memory bandwidth, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs with 32 MB L3 cache
64.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
320 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
Well-suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and caching
